user_task: Aggregation: count / sum / list operations across the retrieved corpus.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, RETRIEVE bucket -> aggregation across the retrieved corpus; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, RETRIEVE -> aggregation ('How many documents are in my knowledge base?'; 'How many <X> docs are in my pile?'; 'Sum <metric> across all <groups>').

success_criteria.quality: operational pass (NOT RAGAS). NRL (the retriever) is TRIGGERED to build/serve the index (`retriever ingest`, optionally `retriever query`), but the COUNT / SUM / LIST-DISTINCT aggregation is performed BY THE AGENT deterministically over rows (LanceDB table rows or retrieved hits) — NOT hallucinated from an LLM summary. The trace must show an explicit aggregation operator (Counter/len/set/sum over rows). Binary pass/fail: the numeric/list answer EXACTLY matches the ground truth computed from the fixtures.
success_criteria.time: RETRIEVE <= 1 min per case (small ingest + one deterministic aggregation one-liner; no agentic multi-hop).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'how many documents / count / sum / list-distinct across my corpus' prompt must fire the skill and drive `retriever ingest`/`query`, not native `ls`/`find`/`wc`.
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — `retriever ingest <paths>` to build the index, then a deterministic LanceDB aggregate one-liner (the canonical references/query.md 'Corpus-level aggregate' snippet) and/or `retriever query` over the served index. No --input-type flag (does not exist).

environment.format_autodetect: `retriever ingest <paths>` auto-detects PDF from the extension; a folder/multi-path ingest produces branch_summary pdf:N. There is NO --input-type flag.
environment.operational_pass_definition: Operational pass = NRL is TRIGGERED (the retriever builds/serves the index) AND the aggregation is AGENT-COMPUTED over rows, not guessed from a summary. The defining check is that an aggregation operator (count = len/Counter, list-distinct = sorted(set(...)), sum over numeric cells) is VISIBLE in the trace and operates on LanceDB rows or retrieved hits — and the result matches ground truth EXACTLY.
environment.canonical_aggregate_snippet: From references/query.md ('Non-semantic operations -> Corpus-level aggregate'): `<RETRIEVER_VENV>/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; from collections import Counter; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); names=[json.loads(s)['source_name'] for s in df['source']]; print(sorted(set(names))); print(dict(Counter(names)))"`. references/query.md explicitly says: for 'list distinct sources' / 'count chunks per source' use this LanceDB one-liner — no `ls`/`grep`/`find`.
environment.row_schema: Each LanceDB row's `source` is a JSON string; `json.loads(source)['source_name']` is the file basename (e.g. 'table_test.pdf'). `json.loads(metadata)['page_number']` and `json.loads(metadata)['type']` (text|table|chart|image|infographic) are available for typed/page aggregates.
environment.query_hit_shape: JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int.
environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.

fixtures_ground_truth.three_pdf_corpus: cases that ingest woods_frost.pdf + table_test.pdf + multimodal_test.pdf -> branch_summary pdf:3; distinct source_name set = {'multimodal_test.pdf','table_test.pdf','woods_frost.pdf'}; COUNT distinct documents = 3.
fixtures_ground_truth.table_test_pdf: 1-page grid: header row 'Year | Bill | Amy | James | Ted | Susan' then 20 data rows for years 2023..2004 (descending). Some cells are 'N/A'. Cross-checks vs catalog: James 2019 = 978, Susan 2023 = 970.

table_test_computed.ted_is_full_column: Ted has a value in all 20 year rows (no N/A) -> Ted column SUM = 14806 is the clean, unambiguous SUM target (no missing-cell handling needed).

cases.title: COUNT documents in the index (count distinct source_name -> N)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the RETRIEVE-aggregation core (an aggregate computed over the retrieved/ingested corpus) in its simplest COUNT form: 'how many documents are in my knowledge base?'. The agent triggers NRL (ingest the folder -> index), then COUNTS distinct source_name over LanceDB rows (len(set(...))) — NOT a number guessed from a summary. Rung 1 is the floor: a single count operator over rows. Adds nothing over a prior rung.
cases.prompt: I just loaded a folder of PDFs (data/) into my knowledge base. How many documents are in it? Count it from the index, don't just eyeball the folder.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); names=[json.loads(s)['source_name'] for s in df['source']]; print(len(set(names)))"

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3, M >= 3)
expected_output.aggregation: len(set(source_name over rows)) = 3 — an explicit COUNT operator over LanceDB rows

pass_when: NRL is triggered: `retriever ingest data/` builds the index with branch_summary pdf:3 (NON-ZERO rows)
the count is AGENT-COMPUTED over rows (len/set of distinct source_name from the LanceDB table), visible in the trace — NOT inferred from an LLM summary or a folder listing
answer is EXACTLY 3
no native `ls`/`find`/`wc` substituted for the corpus aggregate; no --input-type flag

cases.title: LIST distinct sources/types across the corpus (list-distinct over rows)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the LIST flavor of the aggregation task ('list distinct sources'). Adds over rung 1 exactly one dimension: the operator changes from COUNT (a scalar) to LIST-DISTINCT (an enumerated, ordered set) — sorted(set(source_name)) over rows. Same 3-PDF corpus and same NRL trigger; the only new thing is producing the exact distinct membership, not just its cardinality. Still agent-computed over rows, not summarized.
cases.prompt: List the distinct source documents in my knowledge base (the data/ folder I loaded). I want the actual filenames, pulled from the index, not a description.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); names=[json.loads(s)['source_name'] for s in df['source']]; print(sorted(set(names)))"

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3)
expected_output.aggregation: sorted(set(source_name over rows)) — an explicit LIST-DISTINCT operator over LanceDB rows

pass_when: NRL is triggered: `retriever ingest data/` builds the index (branch_summary pdf:3, NON-ZERO rows)
the distinct list is AGENT-COMPUTED over rows (sorted(set(source_name))), visible in the trace — NOT a guessed/summarized list
answer is EXACTLY the set {multimodal_test.pdf, table_test.pdf, woods_frost.pdf} (3 distinct sources)
no native `ls`/`find` substituted for the corpus list-distinct

cases.title: COUNT rows/chunks per source (group-by count over rows)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the per-group COUNT flavor ('how many <X> chunks per doc'). Adds over rung 2 one dimension: the aggregate becomes a GROUP-BY (Counter over source_name) producing a per-source chunk count, not a flat distinct list. This is the canonical references/query.md 'count chunks per source' aggregate. Same 3-PDF corpus / NRL trigger; new dimension = grouping + counting rows within each group, still deterministic over the LanceDB table.
cases.prompt: For my knowledge base (the data/ folder), how many chunks did each document contribute? Give me the per-document chunk counts from the index.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; from collections import Counter; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); names=[json.loads(s)['source_name'] for s in df['source']]; print(dict(Counter(names)))"

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3)
expected_output.aggregation: Counter(source_name over rows) -> {source_name: chunk_count} group-by COUNT over LanceDB rows; sum of the per-source counts == total row count M
expected_output.answer: a per-document chunk count dict over exactly 3 keys {multimodal_test.pdf: c1, table_test.pdf: c2, woods_frost.pdf: c3}; the three counts sum to the total ingested row count M (the exact c1/c2/c3 depend on the live extraction profile — the gradable invariant is: 3 keys, each count >= 1, and the counts sum to M)

pass_when: NRL is triggered: `retriever ingest data/` builds the index (branch_summary pdf:3, NON-ZERO rows)
the per-source count is AGENT-COMPUTED via a group-by over rows (Counter over source_name), visible in the trace — NOT a guessed split
the result has EXACTLY 3 source keys, each chunk count >= 1, and the per-source counts SUM to the total ingested row count M
no native shell tooling substituted for the LanceDB group-by
cases.known_caveat: Per-source chunk counts (c1/c2/c3) depend on the live chunking/extraction profile (text-chunk size, whether table-structure is on), so the suite does not hard-code them. The deterministic, gradable invariants are the GROUP-BY shape: exactly 3 source keys and counts summing to the total row count M reported by the ingest line. (For an exact-number rung, see rung 4's SUM.)

cases.title: SUM a numeric column from table_test (exact total, agent-computed over rows)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the SUM flavor ('sum <metric> across all <groups>') with EXACT arithmetic. Adds over rung 3 one dimension: the aggregate is now a numeric SUM over a table column's cell values (not a count of rows). The agent triggers NRL to extract/index the table, then materializes the column's numeric cells (table rows / page-elements text) and SUMS them deterministically with Python. New dimension: numeric reduction over extracted table cells; the answer is an exact integer that MUST be agent-computed, never an LLM mental-arithmetic guess.
cases.prompt: Ingest data/table_test.pdf and give me the SUM of Ted's entire column across every year in the table. I need the exact total, computed — not estimated.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:1; table rows present with --use-table-structure)
expected_output.aggregation: sum() over the 20 extracted Ted-column cell values — an explicit numeric SUM operator over table rows

pass_when: NRL is triggered: `retriever ingest data/table_test.pdf` (with --use-table-structure so the column cells materialize as table rows) produces NON-ZERO rows
the SUM is AGENT-COMPUTED over the extracted cell values (Python sum over rows/cells), visible in the trace — NOT an LLM mental-arithmetic guess from a summary
answer is EXACTLY 14806 (Ted's full 20-row column; Ted chosen because it has no N/A cells, so no missing-value handling is needed)
fails if the total is asserted without a visible summation operator over the extracted cells, or if the wrong cells are summed
cases.known_caveat: Ted is deliberately the SUM target because its column has a value in all 20 year rows (no N/A), making the exact total (14806) unambiguous. Summing Bill/Amy/James/Susan instead requires dropping N/A cells (present-only sums: Bill 11217 over 15, Amy 9211 over 12, James 13237 over 18, Susan 14985 over 18). If table-structure flattens the grid, the agent can recover the column from the pdfium page-elements text extract and still sum deterministically.

cases.title: Acceptance: corpus-level aggregate answered EXACTLY, aggregation done over rows (operator in trace) with NRL triggered
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the RETRIEVE-aggregation row. Adds over rung 4: a single end-to-end task that requires (a) NRL TRIGGERED to build/serve the index over a multi-doc corpus, AND (b) TWO aggregate operators chained — a corpus-level COUNT (distinct documents over rows) and a numeric SUM (a table column) — BOTH agent-computed deterministically over rows with the operator visible in the trace, BOTH matching ground truth EXACTLY. This is the gate the others build up to: it proves the count/sum is computed-not-hallucinated end to end.
cases.prompt: Build a knowledge base from the data/ folder, then answer two things straight from the index: (1) how many distinct documents are in it, and (2) the exact sum of Ted's column in table_test.pdf across all years. Compute both from the index/rows — do not estimate either number.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER_VENV/bin/python -c "import lancedb,json; df=lancedb.connect('./lancedb').open_table('nemo-retriever').to_pandas(); names=[json.loads(s)['source_name'] for s in df['source']]; print('distinct_docs=', len(set(names)), sorted(set(names)))"

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3; table rows present)
expected_output.aggregation_count: len(set(source_name over rows)) = 3 — COUNT operator over LanceDB rows
expected_output.aggregation_sum: sum() over the 20 extracted Ted-column cells = 14806 — SUM operator over table rows
expected_output.answer: (1) 3 distinct documents (multimodal_test.pdf, table_test.pdf, woods_frost.pdf); (2) Ted column SUM = 14806

pass_when: NRL is triggered end to end: `retriever ingest data/ --use-table-structure` builds the index (branch_summary pdf:3, NON-ZERO rows) — the retriever, not native tooling, serves the corpus

BOTH aggregates are AGENT-COMPUTED over rows with the operator VISIBLE in the trace: distinct-document COUNT (len(set(source_name))) and Ted-column SUM (Python sum over extracted cells) — NEITHER hallucinated from a summary
distinct-document COUNT is EXACTLY 3 (set = {multimodal_test.pdf, table_test.pdf, woods_frost.pdf})
Ted-column SUM is EXACTLY 14806 (20 year rows)
fails if either number is asserted without a visible aggregation operator over rows, or if either value is wrong
